qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: XSS via unsanitized HTMLElement in html() method bypasses DOMPurify protection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Although this finding targets the same repository (jsPDF v4.0.0), it describes a completely different vulnerability. The advisory describes a DoS vulnerability through unvalidated BMP dimensions in the BMPDecoder when processing images via addImage() or html(). This finding describes an XSS vulnerability through unsanitized HTMLElement input to the html() method that bypasses DOMPurify. The root causes are entirely different: missing dimension validation in BMP parsing vs. insufficient sanitization of HTMLElement attributes. The affected code paths (BMPDecoder vs. html() DOMPurify bypass), security consequences (DoS vs. XSS), and CWE categories (CWE-400/CWE-770 vs. CWE-79) are all distinct.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a Denial of Service vulnerability in jsPDF's BMPDecoder component where unvalidated BMP dimension headers lead to excessive memory allocation. The sole qualifying finding describes an XSS vulnerability in jsPDF's html() method through DOMPurify bypass with HTMLElement input. The finding does not mention BMP parsing, dimension validation, the BMPDecoder component, or memory exhaustion as the security impact. The advisory root cause (missing dimension validation in BMP parsing) is distinct from the finding's root cause (DOMPurify bypass).
evidence: Advisory describes jsPDF BMPDecoder DoS via unvalidated BMP dimensions (CWE-400, CWE-770)
Advisory affects addImage() and html() methods when processing BMP images with large header dimensions
xss-via-html-element finding describes XSS in jsPDF html() method via DOMPurify bypass (CWE-79)
No finding mentions BMP parsing, dimension validation, BMPDecoder, or memory exhaustion impact
Different root causes: missing dimension validation in BMPDecoder vs. DOMPurify bypass in html() method
